landing.heroLead: — The NEW, advanced mind-mapping software and Ultimate Research and Learning Tool. Drop any PDF and watch the magic happen — With AI you can extract every concept, relationship, and hierarchy into a beautiful, interactive mind map, on an INFINITE canvas. Then search across everything you've ever mapped. All on the safety of your own machine.
landing.philosophyQuote: Think of the path you've already travelled. Every action, every insight, every small step has been leading here. Marvex Studio doesn't ask you to choose a new direction — All we ask is to be given the opportunity to help you in the inevitable continuation of the trajectory you've set. Progress feels effortless when our advanced tech aligns with your own story, your own battles and struggles and your own ambitions. Stopping now would contradict the very path you've already claimed as your own.

magicMap.title: Drop a PDF · Get a map
magicMap.body: In under 60 seconds, the AI of your choice extracts every key concept, relationship, and hierarchy from any paper or textbook, then renders them as a stunning, interactive map you can zoom, pan and explore.

compileToPdf.title: Maps → Polished documents
compileToPdf.body: Inverse of PDF→Map. Pick a map element, a sub-tree, or the whole map; the AI weaves it into a coherent essay, briefing, or outline. Export the result as PDF or Markdown — your map becomes a publishable draft, no copy-paste required.

secondBrain.title: Every map · One search
secondBrain.body: One search bar to rule them all. Query every mind map you've ever created. Find connections between papers you didn't know existed — even six months later.

localFirst.title: Your key · Zero cloud
localFirst.body: Bring your own AI key — your key, your bill, your data. Maps live in your browser. No accounts. No tracking. No data harvesting. Full offline support.

smartReader.title: Built-in PDF Reader
smartReader.body: Open any PDF link from a map element and the integrated Reader takes over — highlight passages, ink margins, and send selections straight to the map you're building. Your sources, your annotations, all under one roof.

openAnything.title: Open anything · the right way
openAnything.body: Attach any file or URL to a map element. PDFs open in the Reader. Songs launch in your music app, videos in your player, websites in your default browser — every link respects how your OS already loves to work.

calendar.title: Reminders on a real calendar
calendar.body: Tag a map element with a date and it lands on a beautiful calendar view — every map's deadlines, talks, and follow-ups in one place. Local, private, no Google account required.

bookmarks.title: Chrome bookmarks → reading list
bookmarks.body: Export your Chrome bookmarks once and Marvex Studio turns the entire folder hierarchy into a navigable map. Years of saved links, finally organised.

cloudPush.title: Push to Drive · Dropbox · Zotero
cloudPush.body: One click to mirror a map to Google Drive, Dropbox, or your Zotero library — as PDF, PNG, SVG, Markdown, or a full binary attachment. Your call, every time.

one.title: Drop your PDF
one.body: Drag and drop any research paper, textbook, or document. Up to 25 MB.

two.title: Magic happens
two.body: In under 60 seconds, with an AI mind map generator, you can extract every concept, theme and relationship into a visual map.

three.title: Edit & explore
three.body: Drag map elements, edit titles, add branches, zoom and pan. Your map auto-saves locally.

landing.desktopLead: Open the studio and start mapping. It opens instantly, works offline, and never sends a byte of your research anywhere it shouldn't go.

footer.affiliateDisclosure: Marvex Studio is a participant in the Amazon Services LLC Associates Program and other programs. Outbound links may earn us a small commission. This ensures we can maintain our low and accessible pricing policy for our clients.

userBadge.manageSubscriptionHint: Manage subscription, update card, or cancel
userBadge.portalUnavailable: Customer portal unavailable. Email support@marvex.app and we'll handle it within 24h.

empty.title: Your canvas awaits.
empty.body: Start with a blank map, or drop a PDF and let Claude turn it into a beautifully structured mind map in seconds.

modals.searchPlaceholder: Search every map, every map element…

todo.title: Your sticky notes
todo.emptyBody: No sticky notes yet. Insert one from the canvas toolbar to start your to-do list.

index.title: Master Marvex Studio in minutes.
index.lead: Four short, hands-on tutorials that take you from a blank canvas to a full research workflow. No fluff, no filler — just the moves that matter.
index.takeInteractiveTourHint: A 60-second spotlight walk-through inside the studio.

welcome.title: Welcome to Marvex Studio
welcome.body: This quick 60-second tour highlights the four things you'll use most. Skip it at any time and return later from the Learn page.

sidebar.title: Your launchpad
sidebar.body: Start a New map, Open an existing one, or jump into PDF Studio, Library, and the Reader. Every tool you need lives in this sidebar.

canvas.title: The canvas
canvas.body: Drag, zoom, and pan freely. Double-click a map element to rename it. Press Tab to add a child, Enter to add a sibling. Right-click for more options.

toolbar.title: Toolbar
toolbar.body: Undo · Redo · Shape · Export · Screenshot · Cloud Save. Hover any icon for its tooltip, or press ? any time for the full cheat-sheet.

share.title: Save & share
share.body: No save button — every change auto-saves locally. Click Share to copy a read-only link, or use Cloud Save (Pro) to push to Drive, Dropbox, or Zotero.

powerMoves.title: Bonus: 3 power moves
powerMoves.body: Shift-click two-or-more map elements, right-click, and pick 'Group as branch' to pull scattered ideas under one parent — or 'Join with line/arrow' to connect them visually. The 3-minute tutorial below shows it end-to-end.

firstMap.title: Create your first mind map
firstMap.subtitle: From blank canvas to a finished mind map in under five minutes.
firstMap.intro: Every mind-map in this app starts from a single central map element. In this walk-through you'll create a new map, add branches, customise their shape and colour, and save it — all using just a few keyboard shortcuts. By the end you'll know every move you need to run Marvex Studio day-to-day.

step1.heading: Step 1 — Open the Studio
step1.body: Click the Marvex Studio button in the left sidebar, or press ⌘/Ctrl + N to create a brand new map. The canvas fills the screen and your cursor is already on the central map element, waiting for you to rename it.

step2.heading: Step 2 — Rename the centre map element
step2.body: Double-click the map element to enter edit mode, type your topic, and press Enter. Short, punchy titles beat long sentences — this is the root of your tree so treat it like a chapter heading.

step3.heading: Step 3 — Add child branches
step3.body: Press Tab to spawn a new child under the selected map element, or Enter to add a sibling. Hold Shift + Enter to add a sibling above. Keep going until your first ring of ideas is in place.

step4.heading: Step 4 — Style with right-click
step4.body: Right-click any map element to open the context menu. Pick a shape (rectangle, ellipse, diamond, hexagon, cloud, star, pill), a fill colour from the palette, or switch to a bolder stroke. The cosmic presets match the rest of the app beautifully.

step5.heading: Step 5 — Pan, zoom, focus
step5.body: Hold Space + drag to pan the canvas. Scroll or pinch to zoom. Press F to frame the selected map element. Press ? at any time to open the full keyboard cheat-sheet.

step6.heading: Step 6 — Save & share
step6.body: There's no save button — every change auto-saves locally the instant you make it. Click Share in the top bar to copy a read-only link, or use the Cloud Save toolbar button to push to Google Drive, Dropbox, or Zotero (Pro).

firstMap.tip: Pro tip: the Cmd/Ctrl + Z undo history holds 50 steps. You can experiment fearlessly.

pdfStudio.title: Turn a PDF into a mind map
pdfStudio.subtitle: Drop a paper, let Claude structure it, then refine the result by hand.
pdfStudio.intro: PDF Studio is the heart of Marvex Studio's research workflow. You hand it any research paper, textbook chapter, or document up to 25 MB, and an AI pass extracts every concept, theme, and relationship into a structured map you can edit. This tutorial covers the full pipeline — upload, pick a mode, review, refine, and merge with an existing map.

step1.heading: Step 1 — Open PDF Studio
step1.body: Click the purple PDF Studio button in the sidebar. You land on the Intake queue — a dashboard that tracks every document you've processed. Drag-drop a PDF onto the upload zone or click to browse.

step2.heading: Step 2 — Choose a conversion mode
step2.body: Quick Outline parses headings and structure heuristically — fast and free. AI Analysis (default) sends the full text to Claude Sonnet 4.5, which produces a semantic map rich with relationships and annotations. Pro users get unlimited AI runs; free users get three per month.

step3.heading: Step 3 — Review the draft
step3.body: Within 30-60 seconds the draft appears in the preview pane. Scroll through the map element tree — every branch maps to a concept cluster from the paper, and every leaf carries a short summary. If a branch looks off, click it to flag it.

step4.heading: Step 4 — Fix & enrich
step4.body: Use the inline edit tools to rename, merge, split, or delete map elements. Right-click any map element and pick 'Enrich with AI' to ask Claude for a deeper gloss, an example, or a contrary perspective. The original PDF stays pinned alongside the map so you can cross-reference quickly.

step5.heading: Step 5 — Commit to a map
step5.body: Once the draft looks right, click Save as New Map or Merge Into Existing. Merging respects your existing structure and inserts the new concepts as a fresh sub-tree you can reorganise later.

step6.heading: Step 6 — Skim with the Reader
step6.body: Open the Reader from the sidebar to read the PDF side-by-side with its map. Highlight any phrase in the PDF and Marvex Studio will automatically add it to the map as a citation map element — perfect for building up evidence trails across papers.

pdfStudio.tip: Pro tip: batch-upload up to ten PDFs at once and the queue will process them in parallel.

aiResearch.title: Use the AI Research Assistant
aiResearch.subtitle: Ask any question across every map you've made. Get a grounded answer with citations.
aiResearch.intro: Your Second Brain isn't just a pretty tree — it's a searchable knowledge base. The AI Research Assistant pulls from every map, PDF, and highlight you've ever made, then answers your question with Claude Sonnet 4.5 grounded in that corpus. This tutorial covers asking questions, following citations, saving research notes, and Deep Research mode.

step1.heading: Step 1 — Open Research
step1.body: Press ⌘/Ctrl + K from anywhere in the app, or click the magnifying glass in the sidebar. The Research panel slides in from the right with an empty question box.

step2.heading: Step 2 — Ask in plain language
step2.body: Type a specific question. 'What does Popper say about falsifiability?' beats 'Popper'. The more precise your prompt, the tighter the citations. Press Enter to submit.

step3.heading: Step 3 — Read the streaming answer
step3.body: Claude streams the response token-by-token. Every claim comes with a citation chip — hover to see the source snippet, click to jump to the exact map element or PDF highlight it's drawn from. Nothing is invented — if the corpus doesn't know, the assistant says so.

step4.heading: Step 4 — Toggle Deep Research
step4.body: For complex questions flip the Deep Research switch in the panel header. The assistant chains multiple searches, explores relationships, and builds a longer, better-structured answer. It takes longer but handles multi-step questions beautifully.

step5.heading: Step 5 — Save the response
step5.body: Click 'Save to Memory' to pin the answer — it becomes a research note accessible from the /memory page. Subsequent questions automatically consider your saved notes as context, so your assistant gets smarter the more you use it.

step6.heading: Step 6 — Drop into a map
step6.body: Every paragraph of the answer has a quick-add button. Click it to insert that paragraph as a new map element on your current map, with the citation preserved as a sub-note. Your map grows organically alongside the conversation.

aiResearch.tip: Pro tip: ask follow-up questions in the same session to keep Claude's context. Each new session starts fresh.

reorganise.title: Reorganise a sprawling map
reorganise.subtitle: Pull scattered ideas under a parent branch in two clicks. No deleting, no copy-paste.
reorganise.intro: Maps grow fast. After a research session you'll often end up with eight or ten loose branches that obviously belong together — but moving them one-by-one is tedious. This walk-through shows the fastest path: shift-click to multi-select, right-click to group, rename. Same trick works for joining ideas with arrows or lines.

step1.heading: Step 1 — Spot the loose ideas
step1.body: Open any map that's gotten unwieldy. Look for two-or-more map elements that share a theme but live on different branches — symptoms, side-effects, sources, you name it. These are your join candidates.

step2.heading: Step 2 — Hold Shift and click each one
step2.body: Press and hold Shift, then click each candidate map element. Every map element you click stays in the pool — you'll see a fuchsia dashed outline around it. Release Shift when you're done. Plain click on any map element clears the pool, so keep Shift held until the last one.

step3.heading: Step 3 — Right-click anywhere on the canvas
step3.body: With your pool selected, right-click on any empty canvas area (or on one of the highlighted map elements). The first three options are 'Join N with line', 'Join N with arrow', and 'Group N as branch' — pick whichever matches your intent.

step4.heading: Step 4 — Group as branch
step4.body: 'Group N as branch' detaches each selected sub-tree (with its descendants intact) and re-attaches them under a brand-new parent map element called 'New group' — anchored to your map root. You're dropped straight into rename mode so you can give the branch a meaningful name and press Enter.

step5.heading: Step 5 — Or join with a line/arrow instead
step5.body: If the ideas should connect without changing the tree, pick 'Join with line' (or arrow). A free-floating connector appears between consecutive selections — drag the endpoints later, recolour from the right-click menu, or delete if you change your mind. Lines work between map elements, stickies, clipart, anything.

step6.heading: Step 6 — Compile it into a written brief (bonus)
step6.body: Once the branch is clean, click the scroll icon in the top toolbar (or right-click the new parent → Compile to document). The AI weaves every map element title and summary into a 1-page brief, 3-page report, or 8-page deep-dive — your pick. Save it as PDF straight from the preview, or Copy MD into Notion. The fastest way to turn a brainstorm into a deliverable.

reorganise.tip: Pro tip: select 4+ items and 'Group as branch' will pull them all under one parent in a single click — perfect for mid-PDF pivots when you realise three sections are really one super-topic. Pair it with Compile-to-document and you've got a full first draft in under 60 seconds.
